evals.name: clean-spec-green
evals.prompt: Check whether my spec is still consistent — the spec/ directory in this project holds a complete DDD spec (brief, glossary, flows, use cases, plan, plus data/model.dbml and data/usecases.sql for a small course platform, all aligned).
evals.expected_output: The check-align.mjs harness is actually run against spec/, reports zero errors, and the fixed-format alignment report ends with the ✅ Aligned footer.

assertions.text: The check-align.mjs harness was actually executed (not hand-simulated) against the spec directory
The report uses the fixed format with a mechanical-findings section and ends with the ✅ Aligned footer
No artifact file was modified — ddd-align audits, it never edits

evals.name: renamed-table-drift
evals.prompt: Someone on the team hand-edited our data model and I think the spec drifted. Audit the spec/ directory (it has a glossary whose Enrollment term maps to ERD: enrollments, but data/model.dbml now names that table registrations).
evals.expected_output: The harness is run and the report surfaces AL-01 (glossary term maps to a missing table) and AL-02 (untraced table registrations), routing the fix to ddd-domain / erd-modeler, with the ❌ Drift detected footer.

assertions.text: The check-align.mjs harness was actually executed
An AL-01 error is reported naming both the glossary term and the missing table
An AL-02 error is reported for the untraced registrations table
Each finding's row routes the fix to an owning skill (Fix via column)
The report ends with the ❌ Drift detected footer (not a false green)

evals.name: ghost-usecase-citation
evals.prompt: We deleted a use case from spec/usecases.md last week. Can you verify the rest of the spec is still coherent? (The plan.md still has a task whose Implements: cites the removed UC-003.)
evals.expected_output: The harness is run and reports AL-06 (plan task cites a use case that no longer exists), the report recommends deprecation-over-deletion per the spec format, and routes the fix to ddd-plan / ddd-usecases.

assertions.text: The check-align.mjs harness was actually executed
An AL-06 error is reported identifying the plan task and the missing UC-003
The response notes the spec-format rule that items are deprecated (Status: deprecated), never deleted
The report ends with the ❌ Drift detected footer

evals.name: mechanically-green-vacuous-ac
evals.prompt: Run a full alignment audit on spec/. (The spec is mechanically consistent, but UC-002's only acceptance criterion reads "THE SYSTEM SHALL work correctly and handle all cases properly.")
evals.expected_output: The harness is run and passes (zero mechanical errors), but the judged-findings section flags UC-002's acceptance criterion as vacuous — EARS-shaped but content-free — and routes it to ddd-usecases. The judged stage is not skipped just because the harness was green.

assertions.text: The check-align.mjs harness was actually executed and reported zero errors
A judged (non-mechanical) finding flags the vacuous acceptance criterion on UC-002
The judged finding is routed to ddd-usecases as the owning skill
The mechanical findings and judged findings appear as separate sections of the fixed report format
